text:
# Performance Max Asset Specifications
Platform: google_ads
Level: ad
Topic: asset_specs
Source: https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/17091269?hl=en
Verified: 2026-07-24

Google's current official PMax specification states headline assets allow up to 30 characters, with at least 3 and at most 15 headlines for the relevant non-feed asset-group requirement. Other asset types have separate requirements.

Rocky must retrieve current specifications before producing a final executable creative manifest. Never generalize one asset type's limits to another format.

# Google Responsive Search Ads
Platform: google_ads
Level: ad
Topic: responsive_search_ad
Source: https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/docs/responsive-search-ads/get-responsive-search-ads
Verified: 2026-07-24

Responsive Search Ads contain multiple headline and description assets that Google can combine. The API exposes RSA headlines and descriptions on the ad resource.

Create materially distinct, truthful assets tied to search intent. Never fabricate claims, prices or credentials. Validate exact current asset limits before submission.

# Google Keywords and Negative Keywords
Platform: google_ads
Level: ad_group
Topic: keywords
Source: https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/2453972
Verified: 2026-07-24

Negative keywords exclude unwanted search intent. Google documents broad, phrase and exact negative matching and notes that negative matching differs from positive matching.

Rocky must derive keyword themes from the real offer and search intent, separate materially different intent, and exclude clearly irrelevant meanings. Never invent search volume, CPC, competition or forecasts; use live Keyword Planner/account data for numerical evidence.

# Google Performance Max Asset Groups
Platform: google_ads
Level: asset_group
Topic: pmax_assets
Sources:
- https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/17091269?hl=en
- https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/14530785
Verified: 2026-07-24

Asset groups combine creative inputs and can include audience signals. Signals guide machine learning but do not constrain delivery exclusively to those users.

Build asset groups around coherent business/creative themes. Avoid nearly identical duplication without a measurement reason.

# Google Search Ad Group Structure
Platform: google_ads
Level: ad_group
Topic: search_structure
Source: https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/fields/v22/ad_group
Verified: 2026-07-24

Google Ads ad groups expose bidding, settings, status, relationships and performance data. Rocky should group search intent coherently so ads and landing pages match the underlying intent.

Avoid excessive micro-groups without a relevance, reporting or testing reason.

# Performance Max Search Themes
Platform: google_ads
Level: asset_group
Topic: search_themes
Source: https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/16669486?hl=en
Verified: 2026-07-24

Search themes communicate business and customer search context to Performance Max. They differ from traditional keywords and act as AI guidance.

Use them for useful context that landing pages, feeds or assets may not communicate clearly. Do not treat them as exact Search targeting.

# Google Bidding and Budget Guardrails
Platform: google_ads
Level: campaign
Topic: bidding_budget

Select bidding according to the optimization goal and quality of conversion/value data. Never invent Target CPA or Target ROAS from generic benchmarks.

When reliable historical economics are unavailable, an initial approach may be proposed only as STRATEGY_INFERENCE. Connected account history and live forecasts take precedence over generic assumptions.

# Google Campaign Type Selection
Platform: google_ads
Level: campaign
Topic: campaign_type
Source: https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/2567043?hl=en
Verified: 2026-07-24

Google campaign selection starts from goals and campaign type. Search captures active search demand. Performance Max is goal-based and can access inventory across Google channels using Smart Bidding. Other types serve different inventory and objectives.

Rocky decision order: business outcome → conversion goal → demand state → inventory/creative fit → campaign type. Do not choose PMax merely because it spans channels, or Search when the core job is primarily visual demand creation.

# Google Performance Max
Platform: google_ads
Level: campaign
Topic: performance_max
Sources:
- https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/14530785
- https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/16669486?hl=en
Verified: 2026-07-24

Performance Max is goal-based. Audience signals are suggestions that guide Google AI, not exclusive hard targeting. Search themes provide words and phrases describing likely customer searches and additional business context; they are not traditional Search keywords.

Rocky must never represent audience signals or search themes as deterministic targeting.

# Google Measurement — 2026
Platform: google_ads
Level: measurement
Topic: enhanced_conversions
Sources:
- https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/14664077?hl=en
- https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/10000067?hl=en
Verified: 2026-07-24

Google documents unified enhanced-conversion changes in 2026, accepting user-provided data from website tags, Data Manager and API connections. Consent Mode communicates consent choices and changes supported tag behavior; it is not itself a consent banner.

Rocky must verify measurement state rather than infer it from campaign configuration.

# Google Ads India Tax Operational Note
Platform: google_ads
Level: account
Topic: tax
Country: IN
Source: https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/2375370?hl=en
Verified: 2026-07-24

Google's current India documentation lists CGST 9% + SGST 9% for applicable intra-state treatment and IGST 18% for applicable inter-state treatment, with documented exceptions including eligible SEZ treatment.

This is an operational note, not tax advice.

# Meta Ad Creative Layer
Platform: meta_ads
Level: ad
Topic: creative

Choose creative according to objective, placement context, customer insight and assets that actually exist.

Never invent images, videos, testimonials, discounts, accreditation, customer counts, prices, guarantees or performance claims. Exact placement-specific dimensions, duration and text recommendations should be resolved from current Meta specifications for the selected format before final execution.

# Meta Copy, CTA and Destination
Platform: meta_ads
Level: ad
Topic: copy_destination

Maintain message match across hook, value proposition, proof, CTA and destination. The CTA must correspond to the intended action and the destination must exist.

For lead journeys, distinguish website, form, call and messaging flows where available. Do not fabricate character limits; retrieve current specifications for the exact format/placement.

# Meta Pixel and Conversions API
Platform: meta_ads
Level: ad
Topic: measurement
Source: https://www.facebook.com/business/help/AboutConversionsAPI
Verified: 2026-07-24

Conversions API connects business marketing data to Meta systems for optimization and measurement and supports website, app, offline and messaging event use cases. Meta recommends considering CAPI alongside Pixel for website events.

Rocky must verify event source, desired event, browser/server deduplication where relevant, and downstream signal quality. CAPI is not a privacy-policy bypass. Never claim Pixel/CAPI is healthy without connected evidence.

# Meta Advantage+ Audience
Platform: meta_ads
Level: ad_set
Topic: audience
Source: https://www.facebook.com/business/ads/meta-advantage-plus/audience
Verified: 2026-07-24

Advantage+ audience can treat customer lists, lookalikes, age, gender and detailed targeting as suggestions and expand beyond them when Meta predicts improved performance. Meta documents stricter controls including location, minimum age, language and custom-audience exclusions.

Rocky must distinguish audience suggestions from controls. Never claim an interest suggestion guarantees exclusive delivery. Never invent audience size or Meta interest IDs. Human-readable interests require live platform lookup before execution.

# Meta Conversion Location and Performance Goal
Platform: meta_ads
Level: ad_set
Topic: optimization
Sources:
- https://www.facebook.com/business/ads/ad-objectives
- https://www.facebook.com/business/ads/ad-objectives/traffic
Verified: 2026-07-24

The objective alone does not fully define delivery. Rocky must reason about where the desired action occurs and what performance result the ad set should optimize toward.

The exact options exposed by Ads Manager/API are live platform capability. Validate objective → conversion location → performance goal before generating an executable payload.

# Meta Advantage+ Placements
Platform: meta_ads
Level: ad_set
Topic: placements
Source: https://www.facebook.com/business/ads/meta-advantage-plus/placements
Verified: 2026-07-24

Advantage+ placements can distribute ads across eligible Meta surfaces including Facebook, Instagram, Reels, Stories, Messenger and Audience Network.

Automated placements are a reasonable performance default when compatible with the campaign. Restrict placements only for a concrete reason such as creative compatibility, a deliberate experiment, brand safety, legal constraints or destination limitations. Never claim a placement is universally best for Indian SMBs without account evidence.

# Meta Ad Set Testing
Platform: meta_ads
Level: ad_set
Topic: experimentation

A useful test changes a meaningful variable while preserving enough other conditions to interpret the result. Candidate dimensions include audience approach, offer/message, geography, optimization approach or placement strategy when supported by a real hypothesis.

Do not create many tiny ad sets merely to appear sophisticated. Do not label ordinary edits as controlled experiments.

# Meta Campaign Objectives
Platform: meta_ads
Level: campaign
Topic: objective_selection
Source: https://www.facebook.com/business/ads/ad-objectives
Verified: 2026-07-24

Meta's auction uses the selected objective to pursue the result the advertiser wants. Rocky must map the real business outcome to the closest platform objective rather than choosing from industry alone.

Rules:
- Do not use Traffic when the real primary outcome is measurable leads or sales merely because a website is involved.
- Do not default every service business to Leads without resolving the actual conversion journey.
- Keep the business objective separate from the Meta objective.
- Validate objective, conversion location and performance-goal compatibility against the current platform before API creation.

# Meta Campaign Preflight
Platform: meta_ads
Level: campaign
Topic: preflight

Resolve before creation: business goal, platform objective, geography, conversion location, performance goal, budget ownership, schedule, destination, measurement readiness, creative availability, and applicable restricted/special-category requirements.

Unknown required values are MISSING_DATA. Never fabricate them. Rocky should create campaigns PAUSED for human review by default.

# Rocky Executable Campaign Contract
Platform: cross_platform
Level: system
Topic: execution

Every plan must distinguish VERIFIED_ACCOUNT_DATA, USER_PROVIDED_DATA, OFFICIAL_PLATFORM_RULE, STRATEGY_INFERENCE and MISSING_DATA.

Before API creation, validate every required field, enum and platform ID against the connected API/account. RAG explains rules; live API capability determines what can actually be submitted. Default new campaigns to PAUSED for human review.

# Rocky Advertising Anti-Hallucination Rules
Platform: cross_platform
Level: system
Topic: quality

Never invent audience size, interest IDs, keyword volume, CPC, CPM, CPA, CPL, conversion rate, ROAS, competitor spend, platform forecasts, customer demographics, testimonials, offers, prices, credentials, tracking status or expected results.

Static RAG cannot prove live eligibility, inventory, account restrictions, API support or performance.

# Rocky Three-Level Retrieval Plan
Platform: cross_platform
Level: system
Topic: retrieval

Meta retrieval: campaign/objective + ad-set optimization + audience/placements + ad/creative + measurement + execution rules.
Google retrieval: campaign type/bidding + ad-group or asset-group knowledge + ad/assets + measurement + execution rules.

Use metadata filters for platform + level + topic before semantic ranking. Do not use one generic top-k query for the entire campaign plan.
